title: Fluffy Tangy Malaysian Chinese Broccoli Toast
description: A light, airy toast topped with sautéed Chinese broccoli, a savory-sour sauce, and a touch of honey for sweetness – a crowd‑pleasing low‑calorie snack or breakfast.

ingredients.name: Chinese broccoli (choy sum)
ingredients.notes: trimmed and cut into bite‑size pieces

instructions: Preheat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and lightly coat with a few drops of sesame oil.
Place the whole‑wheat slices in the skillet; toast each side for 1–2 minutes until golden and slightly crisp. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add a splash more sesame oil if needed, then sauté minced garlic, grated ginger, and chili flakes for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the Chinese broccoli pieces, season with a pinch of salt, and stir‑fry for 3–4 minutes until tender yet still bright green.
Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, and honey; pour the mixture over the broccoli and stir to coat. Cook for another 1–2 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Beat the egg in a small bowl and pour it over the broccoli. Gently stir to scramble the egg into the vegetables, cooking until the egg is set but still fluffy.
Top each toasted slice with an even portion of the broccoli‑egg mixture, sprinkle with a few chili flakes if desired, and serve immediately while hot.
